lesson.title: Stop the shot. Don't get blown by.
lesson.body_md:
## What is a closeout?

A closeout is when you sprint back to a shooter once they catch the ball. Do it right and you stop the shot. Do it wrong and they drive past you.

```tip
A closeout is just three things: sprint, slow down, hand up.
```

## The 3 jobs

1. **Get there fast.** Big steps for the first part.
2. **Slow down before you arrive.** Small choppy steps so you can stop.
3. **Take one thing away.** You can't stop the shot AND the drive. Pick one.

```mistake
Running full speed all the way to the shooter. You'll fly right past them.
```

## Read the shooter

```reveal
Q: A great shooter just caught the ball at the wing. What do you take away?
A: Take away the shot. Closeout high with your hand up. Force them to drive into help.
```

```reveal
Q: A weak shooter who loves to drive just caught the ball. What do you take away?
A: Take away the drive. Stay low, stay short, and dare them to shoot.
```

## Test yourself

```quiz
Q: You're sprinting at a great shooter who just caught the ball. Where's your hand?
- Both hands down ✗
- High hand on their shooting side ✓
- Hands behind your back ✗
Why: A high hand makes them think twice. Two hands down is a wide-open shot.
```

```quiz
Q: You're closing out and you're going way too fast to stop. What do you do?
- Fly past anyway ✗
- Stay home and contest from where you are ✓
- Reach in for the steal ✗
Why: A bad closeout is worse than no closeout. Trust your help defender.
```

## Watch out for

```mistake
Running straight up. Your feet skid. The shooter rips past.
Fix: bend your knees on the last few steps.
```

```mistake
Closing out flat (square shoulders). Now they pick any direction.
Fix: angle your hips so they only have one good way to go.
```

```takeaway
Sprint, chop, contest. Pick one thing to take away.
```

## Coach says

```coach
Never both feet flat. Never both hands down.
```

Now try it in practice.
